commit.message:
Don't have monsters pick up two ranged weapons

Monsters who carried a melee and ranged weapon were swapping out the
melee weapon for another ranged one.

Fix a couple of problems with shadow coglin ranged

Shadows were not reliably wielding ranged two weapons; depending on the
order they were checked in, they sometimes ended up with one.

Additionally, when they did have two weapons, they were shooting the
same one twice (and then swapping between attacks).
Support UTF8 characters in clipboard

Handle unicode characters in the monster list pane.

Use unicode-aware functions to calculate the length of a monster
description in the monster list and resize it where needed.

Don't crash if there's somehow only 1 character left for a monster's
name in the monster list. It can't happen at present, but it seems like
an odd ASSERT().

Committer's note: also pulled out a variable for readability.
Checkwhite

Fix Dungeon and Depth wall tiles sometimes using wrong variations

tile_init_flavour should give the same result when called on a tile that
has had its flavour initialize and one that hasn't. However, this wasn't
the case for the way it picked Dungeon and Depth tiles variations. This
problem was very noticable when placing a local vault in wizmode as it
calls tile_init_flavour on every square on the level.

Remove an unneeded call to tile_init_flavour

There was a call to tile_init_flavour in vault_placement::apply_grid
that happened before setting flavour. This call was initialy added to
fix some tiles being completely black in abyss vaults. However, the
abyss sets crawl_state.generating_level to true when placing vaults and
this call only happens when its it to false. Instead the abyss now
handles this in _abyss_fixup_vault.

Fix monsters opening clear sealed doors turning them opaque

If a monster opened or broke a clear door that had been sealed by a
vault warden, you would get a regular open or broken door rather than
the transparent version.

Make gavotte predict harm more accurately

For targetters and penance checks:
* In the case where the monster ends up stopping due to water/lava,
  don't predict harm.
* Predict actor collisions in cases such as flyer-air-non_flyer-lava.
* Predict collisions with monsters out-of-los (which won't move with
  Gavotte)

We do this by simulating the whole gavotte movement with player
knowledge rather than ground truth.

Fixes #5314

Fix some penance edge cases with gavotte

* Do penance even when the victim dies, by moving the penance check
  before damage (this still messages *after* the collision damage).
* Have penance rely on "could you see the monster at the start of the
  turn". This was previously using the player's new position, possibly
  after gavotte movement, and the monster's new position, which led to
  some odd outcomes.
* Last and very much least, give penance for friendly wall walkers
  damaged by an incoming collision from a monster who can't live in
  their wall.
Warn for penance from various non-beam spells

Fix xom scenery changes sometimes not being noticable

When Xom changes scenery, the tile override of the changed squares
wasn't being reset. This would result in the tile not changing for any
squares that had a tile override.

Fix placing a vault in mizmode not marking in view terrain changed

Almost everything that set_terrain_changed does (deleting clouds in
solid features, updating slimey wall tracking, updating mold tracking,
firing terrain changed evens, etc.) still needs to happen for in view
terrain. However, we were excluding calling it for in view terrain
presumably so that the terrain didn't get marked as changed. This isn't
actually a problem though as the terrain changed flag will get removed
when we next call viewwindow anyway and if it actually is a problem, it
would make more sense to fix set_terrain_changed as there are so many
other places that assume it is fine to call it for in view terrain.

Fix _tension_door_closed setting some squares as changed

_tension_door_closed closes a door checks the tension and then restores
the door. However, when closing the door we would mark the square as
change, remove all the clouds in the door, and fire off a terrain
changed even. We were making sure to restore the clouds but the way we
did this was not great for performance. Item and monster detection have
complex interactions with changed terrain and it's unclear if firing off
a terrain chenged even is harmful, so it's probably best not to do this
when the terrain hasn't actually changed.

Make some vaults which rely on stairs transparent

Stairs can occasonally be removed from vaults. If they need
those stairs, it's important to mark them transparent so that
they are included in connectivity checks.

Move pool fixup earlier

Deep vs shallow water affects connectivity, so we should update this
before checking connectivity and closets. We move this into the
post_vault_build function so that it is still used as before by all
game modes.

Create a tag to allow tele closets

Some vaults deliberately place teleportation closets, which
they offer item-based escapes from. Now that we mask such
closets by default, we create a tag to allow this, and apply
it to three existing vaults which intend to provide escapes.

Detect and mask teleport closets during level generation

We have a number of strategies to fix and veto teleport closets
during level generation. They mostly work, but many vaults have either
deterministic or occasional teleport closets built in. Here, we add a
way to explicity search for such vaults. This is far more permissive
than other connectivity checks, allowing traps, transporters, etc as
ways to escape.

Empirically, this mostly finds two class of vault:
* Vaults with a bug, that should have more no-tele squares or some
other fix.
* Vaults which procedurally generate tiles which may get disconnected
in ways that would be hard to avoid. Typical examples are large
expanses of mixed deep and shallow water.

To fix these, we mask found teleportation closets with no_tele_into.
Additionally, to aid debugging of closets, we enable a mapstat
argument which turns these into vetoes.

Support pasting into line input in local tile

This is especially useful for pasting in long vaults in wizmode.

Refactor: use an unwind_var in _cull_redundant_stairs

This makes it clear that we only wish to remove the staircase
temporarily. It also makes restoring the staircase less error prone.

Update the documentation of a parameter of _fixup_stone_stairs

_fixup_stone_stairs no longer turns staircases into hatches

Refactor: rename a confusing parameter of _cull_random_stairs

The replacement feature isn't a hatch anymore

Refactor: rename a confusing parameter of _cull_redundant_stairs

The replacement feature isn't a hatch anymore

Fix _cull_redundant_stairs not clearing flavour from removed stair

If the staircase that got replaced had a tile override, its replacement
would use this tile override making it look like a staricase. This
didn't matter previously as _cull_redundant_stairs was effectively
unused due to an inverted condition.

Fix _cull_redundant_stairs doing nothing

It's check to see if stairs needed culling was inverted which resulted
in it always doing nothing as it was only called when stairs needed
culling.

Fix Gozag shops sometimes looking like walls

Dungeon generation can sometimes generate floors with feat flavour set
to a wall tile. This is fine as feat flavour is ignored on floors.
However, if the player converts one of these floors to a shop using
Gozag, we wouldn't clear feat_idx which is used to restore the feat
flavour after a reload resulting in the shop looking like a wall after a
reload.

Fix the preview of a Ru mutation

Ru upgrades your innate mutations not your normal ones - fix the
mutation preview to account for this.

This could be slightly unclear in the case that the normal mutation
is two levels above the innate one, but this is pretty niche and
certainly better than the current situation where we lie about
increasing the level.

Fixes #3949

Fix angering tentacle segments

This fixes a bug whereby attacking the middle of a friendly eldritch
tentacle caused only that section to appear hostile for one turn,
AFAIC with no mechancial effect. Now tentacles correctly object
to you hitting them anywhere at all.

We do this by generalising get_tentacle_head to work for solo tentacles.
This also fixes the threat assessment of eldritch tentacle segments,
which now inherit from their root like krakens already did.

We fix some callers of get_tentacle_head who were unnecessarily calling
it twice (it was already idempotent, though now this is perhaps more
obvious).

Fixes #2843

Fix ruined levels and vaults not respecting FTILE definitions

After commit c73acac we would clear the flavour from a square when
ruining it to stop, for example, a wall that had been turned into
shallow water from still looking like a wall. However, this resulted in
us clearing the floor flavour (defined by FTILE) even though this still
makes sense for the ruined square. Instead, only clear the feat flavour
as this is the only type of flavour that can cause problems when the
dungeon feature has been changed by ruining the square.

Stop ignition firing through walls

Like the fix to fusillade in 93e8449, ignition was hitting squares
behing walls when hitting wall monsters. Instead, use the standard
machinery to make the explosions flow around walls.

We make an explosion iterator to make that "standard machinery" less
boilerplatey.

Also fix the targetter, which was wrong in a different way (assuming
nothing behind a wall would be affected).

Fix wizmode placed features not using tile variations until reload

The code for creating a dungeon feature in wizmode was setting the
"special" flavour for the square to zero and then relying on
dungeon_terrain_changed calling tile_init_flavour to reinitialize it.
However, after commit 4f1e9c7 dungeon_terrain_changed no longer calls
tile_init_flavour as it no longer needs to call it to handle doors and
calling it would sometimes also cause an info leak.

Fix item method docs

Ldoc was generating item methods with a self param (e.g.
`item:inscribe(param)` notation), when the correct usage is
`item.inscribe(param)`.

Committer's note: extended to a few zero-parameter methods, where
the colon usage actualy works but would fail to do so if there
were any arguments.

Stop fusillade firing through walls

When fusillade hit a wall monster, it hit all the squares around that
wall. We fix this to use the standard machinery to calculate the
affected squares.

Fixes #5282

Prevent teleport closets made of traps

The connectivity checks in dungeon generation treat traps as impassable.
This means that they completely ignore areas made *entirely* of traps.
But the player can teleport into these, so that's not ideal.

We fix this by doing an extra connectivity check, vetoing any areas that
are disconnected from stairs when treating traps as passable.

On a sample run of 400 spider levels (which are by far the largest
offenders because of extreme web density), this causes 26 new vetoes out
of a total of 273 vetoes.

Fixes #5294 (this issue may not look like a region made entirely of
traps, but morally it is - most of the region is a vault, which is
invisible to the connectivity algorithm except for its entrance, which
has a trap.

Fix spurious messages during volcano catchup

When checking whether the player is close enough to hear the volcano,
we need to check whether the player is on the map at all.

Fixes #5290

Suppress an out-of-LoS bolas message

Fixes #5300

Fix some effects being endianness dependent

Some calls to hash32 were passed an array of uint32_t. However, because
hash32 works an an array of bytes, the different byte ordering of
uint32_t between big and small endian platforms would result in it
returning a different value. This mainly only affected graphics such as
the tile used by a ghost or which tile variation was used by a dungeon
feature or the description of a random pandemonium lord. However, it did
affect the seed stability of the "sense surroundings" mutation, although
it is unclear to me if this mutation having seed stability is even
intensional.

Fix messages from portals after excursions

When an excursion was triggered - which can happen in a number of ways
including by viewing another floor - timed portal message were then
being suppressed until the portal timed out.

This was happening because of a fix back in e27d229, which added a guard
to fix a bug with too-early messages using the wrong player coordinates
for a portal message on re-entry. Messages were off when the marker was
initialised, and toggled on when entering the level. But excursions
initialise the marker without firing an entered_level event, leaving
messages toggled off.

We fix this by setting the toggle in init only for initial marker
creation, and thereafter toggling it on level entry and exit.

Fixes #5301

Fix breaking large doors

Monsters weren't breaking down doors if the player was adjacent to the
door but not to the square the monster was opening, leading to infinite
door wars.

Fixes #5309

Committer's note: cosmetic change to any_of, reworded commit.
Track tithing piety

Split out max piety wastage for logging

This splits out piety wasted from a stepdown to that wasted at
max piety, which came up as something that would be nice to
know in analysing the bigger ongoing piety changes.
Track berserker piety

Refactor swiftness and make -swift uncancellable

-swift was a strange exception - a negative status that was dispellable
by cancellation but not sped by dissipation. This was an artifact of how
swiftness was coded - as a single, dispellable, not-negative duration
with an extra attribute to say whether we were in +swift or -swift.

We refactor swiftness so that +swift and -swift are different durations,
which seems an improvement in any case as it means there are fewer
exceptions for swift dotted around the code. We then make -swift
uncancellable. This feels the more natural way to resolve the
inconsistency, because it is has the feeling of a "cooldown" at least as
much as a status.

This also fixes a probably-unexpected interaction whereby Mara cloning
a player with -swift produced a clone with +swift.
Update credits

Fix coglin hunters saying farewell on turn 1

Coglins unequip their starting bow while making their starting
equipment. Suppress a message from this, using the existing
parameter that only this call sets.

Fixes #5308

Fix tentacle check to use appropriate function

This uses a more usual function for the fix in 2dfc88a, and in doing so
fixes another issue where friendly tentacles outside the player's LoS
will target but then not be able to hit monsters.

Confirm bump firing with !f inscriptions

When the player attacks by moving into a visible monster while wielding
a ranged weapon, we now warn for inscriptions like we do when fired
through other means.

Change solo tentacles only to attack targets in our LoS

This fixes a rather annoying behaviour where malign gateway will attack
monsters out of our LoS, if they are closer than monsters in our LoS.

This happens because tentacles ignore their foe, instead targetting the
nearest enemy to their current head.

Refactor passage of golubria portal selection

The previous method of selecting one passage then the other could fail
even when there was a valid placement, if the first passage took the
only valid position for the second one.

Committer's note: fixed a bug where we didn't actually abort the cast
on insufficient space, and used some radius_iterators.

Empower the twins even if out of LoS

We were waiting for Dowan/Duvessa to be able to see the player before
powering up when the other twin died. This caused them not to upgrade
if the player was invisible - and part of Dowan's upgrade was being
done early to account for this case. Now we upgrade them immediately
unless they are frenzied.

Also remove a check that the player can see the monster from both elven
twin and Boris orb empowerment. If these monsters are somehow invisible,
they shouldn't be punished for that.

Don't create star jelly wretches (Undo)

5c880ef introduced some code preventing Kikubaaqudgha from offering up
spriggan druid wretches due to their might-animals-on-death effect. This
commit extends it to exclude star jellies, as actively killing them to
raise undead will also let them fire off shooting stars (though if they die
naturally a few turns later, it'll be fine). This is a far more mean result
than is necessary for a god power's random rolls, and there's plenty of
other variety possible for wretches with 0.34's pass on the Slime Pits
anyway.

Fix some out of view door info leaks

If an out of view door that the player had seen was covered in
temporary terrain, it would often change to look like part of a large
door. This was caused by use storing which sides of the door connect to
other doors in the "special" flavour for the square which is used for
other things for other types of terrain.

Another problem was that if the player had seen a door that was covered
in temporary terrain and moved so that it was out of view, when the
temporary terrain ended it would often change which tile variant it
used. This was caused by the "special" flavour for the square being
updated when the terrain reverted back to a door.

There was also a problem where temporary terrain covering part of a
large door wouldn't update what sides the rest of the door showed as
connected on until you reloaded the game. This would result in the
player not knowing that there was a change to part of a door that was
just out of view unless they reloaded their game.

To fix this, store which sides the player remembers a door connecting on
in the map knowledge instead of storing it in the "special" flavour for
the square and update it when updating the remembered feature in the map
knowledge.

Add a space to menu colour search string

This seperates the prefix and item name in the search string for menu
colouring, allowing for e.g. searches which search for an exact item
name.

Fixes #5303

Committer's note: adjusted commit message and made a readability tweak.

Fix some feeling-safe checks

This fixes a bug introduced in d4fc6e0 whereby some calls to i_feel_safe
where ignoring the non-monster checks.

An example bug this fixes is that autopickup was active when in
dangerous clouds.

Dismiss mosnters case-insensitively in wizmode

This makes it consistent with monster creation.

Update warnings for confused movement (Zigmound)

Before this change, we warned for confused movement in two ways:
- Before rerolling the move, we warned for any movement that might cause
us to attack an ally or go over water/lave with expiring flight.
- After rerolling the move, we warn for the actual move with all the
usual warnings (traps, exclusions etc)

The second of these is a bug, the worst effect of which is to allow the
player to place exclusions all around themselves to walk as normal when
confused.

We fix this so that we get no warnings which depend on where we are
going. We also expand the warnings given before the randomisation, to
warn the player if they might stumble into an exclusion, trap, cloud,
etc. There's no great risk of these being spammy, because confused
movement is quite rare.

Following the path of check_moveto_exclusions, we do this by
generalising various check functions to allow checking multiple squares
rather than just one.

Also make the various checks use map_knowledge rather than the ground
truth. This is rarely important, but if you do primordial nightfall
and then a random teleport you shouldn't get warned about traps you
can't see.

Don't leave unlinked items around after fleeing an apostle challenge

Removing the challenger as you leave the floor was unintentionally being
treated as beating them properly, scheduling a revival fineff that would
never be properly processed and generally leaving them in an invalid state
(since it happened in the middle of leaving the floor).

This would unfortunately bombard the player with constant error messages
about unlinked items every turn they spent on that floor for the rest of
the game.

Thanks for Roxie and Ogregutan for the reports. My apologies that it took
me so long to actually fix this one.
Update credits

Stop mutation catalyst rolling beaks (Emily Bunny)

These were missing from the list of banned body facet mutations.

Buff athames a bit more

While 89500f3 went through and made athames reasonably more common,
they're still relatively uncommon picks due to being worse than rapiers
without fulfilling their conditions and worse than quick blades as one
can try to hold out for finding, which hurts their design intent as
curve-smoothing items meant to support mildly more unique builds. (While
the ceiling of Short Blades characters don't need buffs, quick blades are
still fairly better than them when fulfilling their own particular needs
anyway.)

This commit bumps up athame base damage from 6 to 7, letting them be
strictly better rapiers aside from the swapping penalties and needing
slightly more skill investment, so that bothering with their debuff
conditions is more of interest. It also lets them have brands as often as
daggers do (since brand weapon scrolls are often better spent on quick
blades anyway), with mild bumps up of likelihood for flaming, freezing,
protection, devious, vampiric, and draining brands. This is still a
fairly cautious nudge up, but it should hopefully make them worth
considering more often without letting short blades characters just
ignore the more unique kinds of item / spell / god support they've
accumulated over time.

Tune down OTR, Refridge, lost soul flashes

This is another follow-up to cfa9558, using the updated functionality of
the previous commit to reduce the screen flash intensity for Olgreb's
Toxic Radiance, Ozocubu's Refrigeration, and lost souls replacing another
monster on their death. The first two effects being full screen flashes
is reasonably appropriate for their theming and fullscreen range but can
afford to be far less garishly bright and abrupt each time, and as a large
shift of colour it should still be reasonable to notice coming from
monsters. The lost soul effect should still be replaced entirely, but as
it can be adjusted with a single line change now, it is also subdued more
mildly compared to the other two.

Allow specifying alpha of screen flashes

[Committer's notes: While this adjusts the parameters passed to the flash
in most of its calls, this commit doesn't actually change the alpha of any
of these flashes and is only meant to set up the capacity to change such.
Changes to actually provide less sharp flashes will be done in the
following commit.]

A quick small set of monster spell icon updates

When looking through the icon list for components for future spells'
icons, I was stricken with inspiration for updating some of the very
old monster spell icons, which unnecessarily bled past the standard
26x26 frame. This commit adds a fair number of them, remixing other
components as I usually do:

 * Summon Eyeballs: Denzi's old great orb of eyes tile and the usual
   generic summoning sigil Ontoclasm made, as is also used with these
   other summoning spell icons added here.

 * Summon Drakes: Ontoclasm's current Dragon's Call icon.

 * Summon Scorpions: Denzi's old Summon Scorpions icon.

 * Fire Breath: Sastreii's current fire dragon tile and Denzi's old
   Breathe Fire ability icon.

 * Cold Breath: Sastreii's current ice dragon tile and ontoclasm's
   current white draconian Glacial Breath icon.

 * Spit Poison: Denzi's old Spit Poison icon and ontoclasm's current
   Spit Poison icon.

 * Steam Ball: Denzi's old Steam Breath icon and ontoclasm's current
   Fireball icon.

 * Quicksilver Bolt: SentientSupper's Bolt of Light icon and
   ontoclasm's lightning rod Thunderbolt icon.

Fix temp terrain reverting causing some doors to have the wrong tile

If multiple in view temporary terrain squares reverted at the same time
in Crypt and one of them revealed a door and another revealed terrain
that blocked sight of the door, the door would show the non-crypt tile
until the game was reloaded or the player moved so that the door was in
view.

Fix doors closed by sealing sometimes showing the wrong tile

If a large door was sealed in Crypt, any parts of the door that were out
of sight would show the non-crypt door tile until the game was reloaded
or the player moved so that they were in sight.

Fix crypt doors sometimes using non-crypt tiles

If the player opened or closed a large door in Crypt were some of the
door was out of sight, the out of sight part of the door would show the
non-crypt tile until the game was reloaded or the player moved to by
able to see that part of the door.
Remove unused MAP_EXCLUDED_STAIRS flag from map_cell

Fix walls with torches not animating in local tiles

This was broken in commit 45ea6cc when it added back some special casing
for floors and rock walls but didn't add back the special casing for
their animations.

Revert "Rework lightning rod damage"

This reverts commit d6847357b22e053b920fe82ce22be270941b2ead.

Meant to push this to a branch.

Rework lightning rod damage

The current damage from lightning rod sheet lightning is quite
counterintuitive; it does reduced damage to each enemy according to the
number of tiles hit at the same distance from the player.

This is quite hard to think about, and it means that using the lightning
rod to hit a large area is usually less effective than using it for
single beam damage. It also has fiddly targetting shenanigans where it
is good to decrease the number of squares hit.

We update this so that:
* The main beam does full damage every cast
* Squares off the main beam take half damage

This is intended to incentivise more use of AoE; as long as the player
has sensible targets for the main beam they should want to move the beam
in a wide arc to deal some damage to more targets.

Since this should be a buff for lightning rod, we nudge the single-beam
damage numbers down to compensate.

Also make the displayed damage deterministic.

0.35 changelog update (through e467b9c7cb)

Better late than never, of course.

Loose other minor tile adjustments

 * Misery clouds have had their palette compressed to fit other negative
   energy effect palettes.

 * Mlioglotl's Corruption effect has been tweaked some more, animating
   faster but with a fixed very short pause at the end, to hopefully
   continue to be less annoying as a bound soul.

 * Mild additional rltiles/unused sorting has been done, including moving
   a set of beta poltergeist effect tiles that never ended up being used
   anyway.

Add new Shatter vfx + option to choose either

Shatter's animation has been somewhat controversial over time in its
screenshake effect, especially with the way webtiles is coded versus the
other formats making it shake the slice of the screen corresponding to
the 80x24 console equivalent instead of all one sees in webtiles. This
has gone on so far that some players recommend turning off all player
effects entirely, which reasonably elicits some intervention.

As an alternative, there is now a new bespoke multiple shockwaves
animation, derived from an LRD explosion submission by LemurRobot (which
isn't implemented yet for the many special cases it'd require). One can
opt for choosing either with the new option, `alt_shatter_animation`,
which is set to true on webtiles and false on local tiles or console.
Either way, the walls actually broken by Shatter are now highlighted
after each cast, rather than them spontaneously disappearing outright.

(A third much simpler animation setting for Shatter might be considered
if people who don't turn off all animations think the new effect is
somewhat excessive for a spell cast many times in a row, but really,
level 9 spells should have _some_ fanciness to them for how long it
takes to reach them.)

Since the shillelagh "Devastator" uses the same damage evaluation as
Shatter, it now uses one of Shatter's vfx whenever it hits instead of a
generic earth / acid splash highlight, regardless of the above settings.

Replace a decent chunk of mid-combat screen flashes

Screen flashes meant to actually show up mid-combat (as opposed to effects
like god brands or rune pick-ups or opening the door to Zot) should be
reserved for fairly larger swings in combat like Fear or Paralysis or being
at fairly low health, rather than being very distracting as a default
resort for various. This commit serves as a delayed follow-up to fe3fe7f in
trying to remove various other combat flashes that can just have target
highlighting instead, to both provide more unique vfx and to help pare down
excessive screen colour flashes.

 * Leda's Liquefaction: instead of multiple brown-yellow screen flashes,
   it now highlights every square that'll have the mud appear with layered
   flickering ripples, with a new effect combining some CC0 dirt scraps by
   Surt with CC0 water-and-mud pieces by josehzz.

 * Eringya's Noxious Bog: instead of a green screen flash, it now
   highlights every square that'll have the bog appear with a brief
   couple of faint bursts, with a new faint effect combining CanOfWorms's
   irradiate explosions with the same CC0 dirt graphics by Surt that
   the bog tiles uses as of c4f44f9.

 * The Singing Sword: instead of a yellow screen flash, it now
   highlights every monster hit with a new cartoony music effect
   derived from CanOfWorms's Fugue of the Fallen spell icon. (This
   would normally be a bit out of place, but the item itself has some
   extraordinarily silly singing lines, so it felt appropriate.)

 * Slime creatures merging: instead of a green screen flash, it has
   a new pair of effects on the nearby tiles of the fused slime creature,
   reusing old slime creature tiles by roctavian. (This one is one of the
   ones I'm most worried about, but the new animation's movement display
   should still be reasonably noticeable, and slime creatures are already
   something to play cautiously and slower around anyway.)

 * Drain Life: instead of a black screen flash, it has a quick vacuum
   animation and highlights for players and monsters hit, with a new
   effect akin to Gloom's further remixing CC0 particle effects by
   Kutejnikov.

The remaining main targets for further adjustment this version are
Olgreb's Toxic Radiance and Ozocubu's Refrigeration, which probably could
just resort to fainter transparency in a future commit, and lost soul
revival, which is much rarer than these other effects and wants some
fairly bespoke effects. Of the other major mid-combat effects:

 * Maxwell's Capacitive Coupling has long intervals between each screen
   flash, so I'm not too worried about it,

 * Cause Fear and Call Tide deserve the flash as much as paralysis and its
   friends do,

 * and lurking horror explosions want some settling on Symbol of Torment
   itself having any visual effects first.

Remove spammy resists message

Following the previous commit, it has been pointed out that "X resists"
every turn is somewhat spammy. We print one resistance message anyway
when the monster is initially hit, and there is a punctuation difference
when they resist fire, so the player still gets some feedback.

We keep these messages for the player resisting burning - this is higher
signal/noise ratio. This is consistent with how we message for actors
resisting clouds.

Improve sticky flame messaging.

Add resist messages for player and monster.
Closes #3885.

Committer's note: tweaked the code for the monster versions to improve
readability.

Flip ignore_temp flags to include_temp

This makes them consistent with other such flags.

Rename boolean temp parameters to include_temp

We have many boolean parameters on play attributes that mean
"also include temporary effects". The author finds "temp" a
confusing name for these, as it reads too much like "only temp".
We rename them to "include_temp" for clarity.
Fix monster bot not handling AF_BURSTSHROOM
Fix monster bot not handling unicode monster glyphs properly

Describe attack delay changes on all items

This updates our displaying of attack delays on armour (which was
previously only for the equipped body armour on ranged weapons) to use
the same machinery as AC/EV/SH changes. This makes it display on all
items which change these delays (except weapons themselves), and to
preview changes for unequipped items.

Update @ attack delay to be rounded to the nearest integer

Missed in fe84ade

Separate hidden spells for divine exegesis

Change the divine exegesis menu so that it maintains a separate list
of hidden spells from memorisation.

The player often wants these lists to be very different, as exegesis
uses spells the player will never be able to cast.

Make stash search sort results by type, by default

The current default is distance, which can be moderately relevant when
finding distant items, but in many cases makes it harder to evalute the
results of any search that finds many items at once (especially those that
also contain features, which are irrelevant to larger searches most of the
time).

Now it sorts first by item base type instead.

In many cases, I feel this makes searches easier to parse (especially if
one wants to examine items on their current floor).

Allow 'feature' to match all features when doing stash search

Which also allows '!!feature' to exlude them
Add a comment explaining a choice of delay scale

feat: Add options to limit longwalking aka shift running

This adds a new option, longwalk_range, that gives you some control over
the maximum distance shift+direction can take you. The options are
* <constant> - a positive integer range. This is the default with the
               range being 15.
* los        - travel as far as your character's current line of sight
               range.
* visible    - travel only to tiles you can see when you start.
* unlimited  - no distance limitation (the previous behaviour).

The motivation is that sometimes longwalking can unexpectedly put you
dozens of tiles from where you started in unexplored territory, leaving
a lot of opportunity for monsters to flank you. This rather undermines
one important use case of shift+direction, which is to let the player
explore manually with significantly fewer key presses.

The constant 15 is chosen because that's a full LoS diameter normally.
With a human you'll have walked far enough that no visible tile was
visible 15 steps ago. That seems like a good default cap, without
allowing the player to walk across the entire map by accident. It's also
high enough that it hopefully won't annoy existing players who value the
unlimited range.

Committers note: reworked slightly into a single option, and added an
out of bounds check in the visible path (this did not actually have an
error case but seems better not to rely on downstream OOBs checks).

Delete some dead code

This was a no-op because all it is doing is sometimes continuing as the
last action in a loop.

Also, the check was wrong anyway, because uppercase letters come before
lowercase, not after.

Committer's note: adjusted commit message.
Do not show breath status if can't use it in current form

Create lua func you.movement_cost()

Committer's changes: To make this deterministic, we implement a
deterministically rounded version of the player's movement speed,
which we also use in a couple of other in-game displays which
could previously switch between different displays.

Make movement speed a bit less random

The calculation of movement speed has many factors. Before this
commit, these were applied with aut-scale rounding between each
step. This could lead to a player with complex speed-affecting
statuses having a variety of possible movement speeds - as a simple
example, hasted nagas could take 8, 9 or 10 auts over a move.

We address this by introudcing scale parameters to the relevant
functions, which let us do the calculations with far greater
precision and then randomly round them only once. This means that
in essentially every case, movement happens at one of two adjacent
delays.

Clean up evil conducts a little

is_potentially_evil_item covered the chaos brand, which is
already full blown evil, and chaos ammo. We make the
latter outright evil as well.

Make some forbidden items more findable

God-forbidden items were being marked as permanently useless, meaning
that by default they were hidden in ctrl+f. This makes sense for truly
irredeeemable items, but for some cases either the god or a scroll of
brand weapon may later make the item useful. In these cases, we now only
mark the item as temporarily useless, making it show up in searches.

Fix a monster lookup crash

Splinterfrost barricade lookup via ?/m crashed, since the Splinterfrost
Shell spellpower key isn't set in this case. This commit fixes this by
using a default spellpower of the splinterfrost barricade's default HD,
like we do for other forged or conjured monsters.

Fix rendering of WebTiles descriptions (Mouldy Labs)

In WebTiles, arbitrary HTML could be rendered in the browsers of all
spectators based on player item inscriptions and ancestor names that
were passed through unmodified to the description UI. This commit fixes
this vulnerability by rendering the relevant description titles as
non-HTML text. I've also made similar modifications for the description
UI of dungeon features, spells, cards, generic database lookups, game
seeds, and game versions. These shouldn't contain user-specified input,
but they don't need markup, and it's better to be safe. Probably we
should have a more systematic approach for prevening markup in
user-supplied text from being rendered by the WebTiles client.
Update the credits

Round displayed attack delay to the nearest aut

We were previously rounding down, which is both less accurate and caused
disagreements with the armour penalty (which was already rounding)

Also change the penalty scale used by the armour/shields code to match
the actual one, resolving another small disagreement here.
Don't show encumberance warnings for ineligible species

Add effect of shields to weapon delay description.

Fixes #3474

Display Status Icons on Monster Tile in Title of Describe View

The function DungeonCellBuffer::add_monster() is used once in
describe.cc to display the monster tile when viewing a monster
description, and is used nowhere else. Local Tiles has not displayed
status icons nor foreground flags on the monster tile in the title of
the describe view since commit 322ac23. A call to function
pack_foreground() was removed from add_monster() in that commit,
and that led to issue #5132.

Add a function call to pack_foreground() in
DungeonCellBuffer::add_monster() so that status icons and foreground
flags display on the monster tile in the title of the describe view.
This effectively reverts 322ac23, while incorporating the
tile_with_flags_t refactoring done in 9a5c35d, and fixes #5132.
